folio.label: 185r
folio.l: The pride of man and beest , he leide a doun
Where so he come , vn to the worldles ende
¢ Comparisou~ , mygút neuäe ©et be maked
Bitwix hi~ , and another Conquerour
For al this world , for drede of hi~ hatú quaked
He of knygúthode , and of fredome flour
Fortune hi~ maked , the Eyr of hire honour
Saue wyn & wo~men , no thyngö mygút a swage
His heigú entent , in armes and labour
So was he ful , of leonyn corage
¢ What prys were it to hi~ , thougú I ©ow tolde
Of Darius , and an hundred thousand moo
Of kynges , Pénces , Dukes , Erles bolde
Whicú he co~quered , & brougút he~ in to woo
I seye , as fer , as man may ride or go
The world was his , what shulde I more deuyse
For thougú I wryte , or tolde ©ow euäe moo
Of his knygúthode , it mygút nat suffise
¢ Xij ©er he regned , as seitú Machabe
Philippus sone , of Macedoyne he was
That first was kyngö , in Grece , the Contre
O worthy gentil Alisandre , allas
That euäe shulde fallen , swicú a kas
Empoysenned , of thyne owen folk , æè were
ThyThy thy Sys fortune , hatú tåned in to as
And for the , ne wep she neuäe , a teere
¢ Who shal me ©eue teres , to compleyne
The detú of gentillesse , & of fraunchise
That al this world welded , in his demeyne
And ©et hi~ thougúte , it mygúte nat suffise
So ful was his corage , of hey emprise
Allas who shal me helpe , to endyte
False fortune , & poysou~ to despise
The whicú two , of al this woo , I wyte
¢ By wysdom , manhed , and by gret labour
Fram humble bedde , to real magestee
Vp ros he , Iulius , the Conquerour
That wan al thoccident , by lond and See
By strengthe of hond , or elles by tretee
And vn to Rome , made hem tributarie
And sithe of Rome , the Em¹oå was he
Til æ fortune , wex his aduäsarie
¢ O mygúty Cesar , that in Thessalie
